name: Alembic
what_it_does: Database migration tool for SQLAlchemy. Alembic tracks schema changes as versioned migration scripts, enabling controlled schema evolution with upgrade/downgrade support. Generates migration scripts automatically by comparing current SQLAlchemy models to the database state. The standard migration solution for SQLAlchemy-based Python applications.
use_cases: Manage database schema evolution in Python agent backends with version-controlled migration scripts
Auto-generate migration scripts when SQLAlchemy models change — Alembic diffs models against live DB
Deploy schema changes safely in CI/CD pipelines for agent data stores with rollback support
Handle multi-tenant schema migrations where each tenant has their own schema namespace
Coordinate schema migrations across team workflows with ordered versioned migration scripts
not_for: Non-SQLAlchemy databases — Alembic is tightly coupled to SQLAlchemy; use Flyway or Liquibase for non-Python stacks
Django projects — use Django's built-in migration system instead
Document databases (MongoDB) — Alembic is SQL/relational only
best_when: You're using SQLAlchemy as your ORM in a Python project and need structured, versioned database schema migrations with autogenerate support.
avoid_when: You're not using SQLAlchemy, working with Django (use Django migrations), or need a language-agnostic migration tool (Flyway, Liquibase).

auth.notes: Alembic uses SQLAlchemy connection strings for DB access. Auth is handled by SQLAlchemy driver connection parameters.

pricing.notes: Completely free and open source, maintained by the SQLAlchemy team.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — tool runs migrations against your database

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear error messages for migration conflicts, missing revisions, and database connectivity issues. Migration history errors include revision IDs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Migration scripts are versioned and tracked in alembic_version table — re-running alembic upgrade head is idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Autogenerate does not detect all schema changes — renamed columns, data migrations, custom types, and some constraints require manual migration scripts
The alembic_version table tracks current migration — agents managing migrations programmatically must handle the case where the DB has no alembic_version table yet
Migration order is a DAG — branched migrations (e.g., in separate features) can create merge points that require explicit merge migrations
SQLAlchemy 2.0 requires alembic 1.9+ — version mismatch causes cryptic import errors; ensure compatibility when upgrading
Downgrade scripts are not auto-generated — developers must write rollback logic manually or accept that some migrations are irreversible
Running migrations in production requires careful sequencing with deployment — no-downtime deployments need expand/contract pattern not built into Alembic

security_score_components.security_notes: Connection strings in alembic.ini must not be committed to version control. Use environment variables for DATABASE_URL. Standard SQLAlchemy TLS support.
